description:
Automated translation (refer to source for accuracy)

Hanno City has transitioned to standardized municipal information systems on February 24, 2026 (Reiwa 8), with some functions beginning January 14, 2026 (Reiwa 8).

【Changes】
• Various certificates and mailed documents will use "standard administrative characters" for text design. Names and addresses may appear different from before.
• Some high-volume mailings—including Resident Tax and Prefectural Inhabitant Tax notification forms, Forest Environment Tax notification forms, and National Health Insurance Tax notification forms—will be sent from Kanazawa Central Post Office (Kanazawa, Ishikawa Prefecture), which may delay delivery.

The standardization covers 20 administrative areas: child allowances, child and childcare support, resident registry, family register, fixed asset tax, personal resident tax, light vehicle tax, school enrollment, health management, child dependent allowance, welfare assistance, disability welfare, long-term care insurance, national health insurance, late-stage medical care for the elderly, and national pension.

Source (Japanese): https://www.city.hanno.lg.jp/soshikikarasagasu/sogoseisakubu/johosystem/12499.html
summary: ℹ️ Hanno City adopts standardized municipal information systems
